0.title: When customs became vendor-first
0.lede: From 2018 through 2022 the typical custom shipped through one host vendor and a fan of regional proxies; by 2026 the baseline custom is a configurator on a vendor's own storefront. Boards moved. Keycap group buys mostly didn't.

1.title: What Cherry's MX2A revision actually changed
1.lede: Cherry's MX2A landed in 2023 with a real engineering update — factory lube, tighter tolerances, the same 100M-press lifespan. The reason the discourse cooled isn't that the switch failed; it's that the enthusiast slot it aimed at was already claimed by the time it arrived.

2.title: GMK CYL Prussian Alert opens at KBDfans on May 15
2.lede: GMK CYL Prussian Alert lands at KBDfans on 2026-05-15 with a Bauhaus-leaning red-on-cream palette. The IC has been the loudest GMK CYL thread on GeekHack heading into the buy.

3.title: The split/ergo cohort grew up
3.lede: Split and ergonomic boards spent a decade as a hobbyist sub-cohort orbiting a stagger-majority. By 2026 the Voyager line, the Glove80, and a steady trickle of r/mk transition posts have pushed split/ergo across a visibility threshold — still a minority, but now a reference category instead of a footnote.

4.title: How 75 percent became the default custom layout
4.lede: Two years ago a custom-board recommendation defaulted to 65% with a polite note about 75% as the next step up. The Sonnet 2026 refresh, the QK75, and a crowded mid-tier have flipped that order. 75% is now the layout customs reach for first.

5.title: Reading the Trends Tracker
5.lede: thock's Trends Tracker is a weekly snapshot of what is rising, falling, or holding flat across the hobby — switches, keycaps, layouts, vendors, and brands, scored on a single -100 to 100 scale and updated every Friday. This is what the columns mean and what they do not.

6.title: Pairing Oil Kings with the Mode Sonnet
6.lede: This pairing has been on the bench for months, and a free Sunday finally made it happen. The Sonnet is a soft 65 with a top-mount-leaf gasket, and Oil Kings are a heavy factory-lubed linear — together they hit a register worth coming back to.

7.title: Mode Sonnet R2 opens at CannonKeys
7.lede: Mode has announced the second round of its 65% Sonnet board at CannonKeys. The buy is now scheduled for 2026-06-01 through 2026-07-15 — see the in-article update callout for the dates and accent-weight color options under the post-2026-05-09 schedule.

8.title: The slow fade of Alice layouts
8.lede: Alice mounts had a clear moment between 2021 and 2023 — the splayed alphas, the angled bar, the promise of a typing posture that didn't twist your wrists. The thock trends snapshot for 2026-W19 pegs Alice movement at -18 and trending down. The layout still has loyal users, but the curve has bent.

9.title: Why the Gateron Oil King sounds the way it does
9.lede: Few stock linears earn the word 'creamy' on first press. The Oil King does, and the reason is not a secret stem material or a proprietary lube — it is a careful pairing of housing resins and a factory tune that other linears under thirty cents tend to skip.

10.title: A beginner's guide to picking your first switch
10.lede: Switches are the single biggest factor in how a keyboard feels and sounds, and the catalog has grown overwhelming. This guide narrows the entire market into three families, explains who each one is for, and gives a three-step path from curiosity to a confident first purchase. By the end, the reader can pick a category without second-guessing.
